current_metaculus.title: Will another U.S. cabinet member leave office before May 2026?

token_output.content:
BinaryReport(question=BinaryQuestion(question_text='Will another U.S. cabinet member leave office before May 2026?', id_of_post=43099, page_url='https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43099', id_of_question=43105, state=, num_forecasters=1, num_predictions=3, resolution_criteria='This question will as Yes if, after April 5, 2026, and before May 1, 2026, a member of the Cabinet of the United States leaves office or is announced to leave office.', fine_print='This question will consider the whole cabinet, including the designated cabinet-rank positions. If a position is dropped from cabinet-rank status, it will still count for purpose of this question. Specifically this question will consider the following positions: * President * Vice President * Secretary of State * Secretary of the Treasury * Secretary of Defense * Attorney General * Secretary of the Interior * Secretary of Agriculture * Secretary of Commerce * Secretary of Labor * Secretary of Health and Human Services * Secretary of Housing and Urban Development * Secretary of Transportation * Secretary of Energy * Secretary of Education * Secretary of Veterans Affairs * Secretary of Homeland Security * Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency * Director of the Office of Management and Budget * Director of National Intelligence * Director of the Central Intelligence Agency * United States Trade Representative * Administrator of the Small Business Administration * White House Chief of Staff Acting members will not be considered as members for this question. To qualify, an announcement that a member will leave office without immediate effect must include a set date or period, and that date or period must end before August 2026. If an individual leaves the cabinet due to being nominated for another listed position, that will not resolve this question even if they later fail to reenter cabinet.', background_info='The [United States cabinet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cabinet_of_the_United_States) is made up of the Vice President, the heads of the 15 executive departments, as well as a number of designated cabinet-level officials. As of April 2026, there are 7 additional cabinet-level officials, with various other positions being included in the past. During the first Trump administration, 14 cabinet officials [left office](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_cabinet_of_Donald_Trump), substantially higher than Biden who saw only 2 departures, and Obama who oversaw 8 across two terms. The second Trump administration has seen 2 cabinet members leave office, with Secretary of Homeland Security Kristi Noem [being removed](https://apnews.com/article/trump-homeland-security-noem-mullin-38c583b3cef97b4ef60d84b8f8b5961a) during March 2026 and Attorney General Pam Bondi [being fired](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/02/us/politics/trump-fires-bondi-attorney-general.html) on April 2, 2026. Following the rapid removal of two cabinet officials in less than a month, media speculation has surged regarding other possible exits, [particularly](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/apr/02/trump-tulsi-gabbard-intelligence-chief) Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard.', unit_of_measure='',
